Petition For Second Preliminary Distribution
Off Calendar Sep-24-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Schultz for Herb Thomas (Trustee). Cecelia Fusich for Suzanne Mulcahy and Anita Mulcahy. Richard Collier for Michael Penny.

The Court noted that the writ of attachment is on calendar for September 23, 2014. Ms. Fusich proposed that distribution to the other Beneficiaries go forward and that distribution to Mr. Penny be stayed and placed into an attorney trust account. Mr. Collier stated that he does not see why distribution should be held up, that cutting off interest is like saying that they (Ms. Fusich and her Clients) are right, that there is nothing before this Court and that it is unclear if they have standing. Ms. Fusich once again argued that if distribution is made to Mr. Penny, there won't be enough to settle their claim. The Petition for Second Preliminary Distribution was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

Petition For Second Preliminary Distribution
Off Calendar Sep-02-2014 Continued To Sep-09-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Schultz for Herb Thomas. Cecelia Fusich for Anita Mulcahey and Suzanne Mulcahey.

Ms. Fusich stated that Department 501 required notice, that she is going in today to request shortened time and that the Parties are in settlement conference today. Mr. Schultz raised the issue of damage to the Remainder Beneficiaries, stated that he spoke with Ms. Fusich about a one week continuance and that he asked that the Attorney for the Remainder Beneficiaries appear. The Court stated that is does not see why the Remainder Beneficiaries should be harmed by this. Ms. Fusich stated that she provided notice to Mr. Woo (Mr. Penny's Counsel) about todays hearing. The Court stated that it is inclined to stop the interest on Mr. Penny's bequest as of last week and noted that Mr. Woo is not here to object. The Petition for Second Preliminary Distribution was continued to September 9, 2014 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)

Petition For Second Preliminary Distribution
Off Calendar Aug-26-2014 Continued To Sep-02-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Schultz for Herb Thomas. Cecelia Fusich for Anita Mulcahy, Mary Joe Mulcahy and Suzanne Mulcahy (Objectors).

Ms. Fusich raised the issue of having an evidentiary hearing regarding financial elder abuse, stated that she is filing a writ of attachment and temporary protective order, that she has informed Mr. Woo and Mr. Nam (Mr. Penny's Counsel) and that the matter will be heard this Thursday in Dept. 501. Ms. Fusich also stated that there is a law suit pending in San Francisco (case #530319) and that the amount of the writ is about $300,000.00. Mr. Schultz stated that if Mr. Penny receives distribution, it is receiving interest at this point and will diminish distribution to the others. The Petition for Second Preliminary Distribution was continued to September 2, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of the writ of attachment. Ms. Fusich is to advise Mr. Penny's Counsel.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (2251 Cecilia Avenue, San Francisco, Ca)
Off Calendar Oct-31-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Schultz for Herb Thomas.

The Court noted that it received the re-appraisal. The sale was confirmed at $856,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale filed October 9, 2013. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). An additional bond in the amount of $817,000.00 is required. Mr. Schultz stated that he will be asking for a 30-day continuance on the accounting status hearing in the conservatorship matter, that he would like to have the Trust out from under Court supervision and that the will file an accounting petition in the trust matter. Mr. Schultz also stated that the Trust can not be distributed because there is a property in Florida and it requires an ancillary proceeding.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Whitney. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Dec-19-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with case number PCN-11-294306 Conservatorship of Johnnye Jenious.

Appearances: Andrew Schultz for and with Herb Thomas (Trustee/Conservator). Ruth Sullivan Court appointed Counsel for Johnnye Jenious (Conservatee).

Mr. Schultz addressed the issues raised in the Examiner's notes with regard to Mr. Thomas' fees in the Trust matter, explained time spent, visits, circumstances, and stated that they agree to a reduction of 5% as wells as a reduction of 15.8 hours with respect to the bank deposits. Ms. Sullivan is to remain on as Court appointed Counsel to assist with future planning. The accounting petitions were taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Benton.
